1→/**
     2→ * App Event Catalog
     3→ *
     4→ * Single source of truth for ALL real-time event names and their payload schemas.
     5→ * These events flow through the app via different transports (Electron IPC, stdout
     6→ * relay, Unix socket) but the contracts are defined here regardless of how
     7→ * they're delivered.
     8→ *
     9→ * Every `listen()` call in the frontend MUST use an event name from this file.
    10→ * The Electron main process (apps/desktop/main/) must be kept in sync manually.
    11→ *
    12→ * Adding a new event:
    13→ *   1. Add the event name constant below
    14→ *   2. Define the Zod schema + inferred type
    15→ *   3. Add the mapping to AppEventMap
    16→ *   4. If emitted from main process, update the corresponding handler in apps/desktop/main/
    17→ *   5. TypeScript will enforce correct payload types at all listen() call sites
    18→ */
    19→
    20→import { z } from "zod";
    21→
    22→// ============================================================================
    23→// Event Name Constants
    24→// ============================================================================
    25→
    26→/** Workspace events — backend → main process → frontend */
    27→export const WORKSPACE_PROGRESS = "workspace:progress" as const;
    28→
    29→/** File system events — chokidar watcher → frontend */
    30→export const FS_CHANGED = "fs:changed" as const;
    31→
    32→/** PTY events — node-pty manager → frontend */
    33→export const PTY_DATA = "pty-data" as const;
    34→export const PTY_EXIT = "pty-exit" as const;
    35→
    36→/** Browser automation events — Electron BrowserView → frontend */
    37→export const BROWSER_PAGE_LOAD = "browser:page-load" as const;
    38→export const BROWSER_TITLE_CHANGED = "browser:title-changed" as const;
    39→export const BROWSER_URL_CHANGE = "browser:url-change" as const;
    40→export const BROWSER_WORKSPACE_CHANGE = "browser-window:workspace-change" as const;
    41→export const BROWSER_DETACHED_CLOSED = "browser:detached-closed" as const;
    42→
    43→/** Simulator events — main process → frontend */
    44→export const SIM_BUILD_LOG = "sim:build-log" as const;
    45→
    46→/** Chat insert events — main process → frontend */
    47→export const CHAT_INSERT = "chat-insert" as const;
    48→
    49→/** Backend lifecycle — main process → frontend */
    50→export const BACKEND_PORT_CHANGED = "backend:port-changed" as const;
    51→
    52→/** Git operations — main process → frontend */
    53→export const GIT_CLONE_PROGRESS = "git-clone-progress" as const;
    54→
    55→// ============================================================================
    56→// Domain Constants (queryable resources, mutations, sidecar notifications)
    57→// ============================================================================
    58→
    59→/** Queryable resources — single source of truth.
    60→ *  Derive the type from the const array so runtime validators and
    61→ *  compile-time checks always stay in sync. */
    62→export const QUERY_RESOURCES = ["workspaces", "stats", "sessions", "session", "messages"] as const;
    63→export type QueryResource = (typeof QUERY_RESOURCES)[number];
    64→
    65→/** Mutation action names for the WebSocket relay protocol (sync data writes). */
    66→export const MUTATION_NAMES = ["archiveWorkspace", "updateWorkspaceTitle"] as const;
    67→export type MutationName = (typeof MUTATION_NAMES)[number];
    68→
    69→/** Command names for the WebSocket relay protocol (async actions). */
    70→export const COMMAND_NAMES = [
    71→  "sendMessage",
    72→  "stopSession",
    73→  // PTY commands
    74→  "pty:spawn",
    75→  "pty:write",
    76→  "pty:resize",
    77→  "pty:kill",
    78→  // File system commands
    79→  "fs:watch",
    80→  "fs:unwatch",
    81→  // Browser server commands
    82→  "browser-server:start",
    83→  "browser-server:stop",
    84→  // Git commands
    85→  "git:clone",
    86→] as const;
    87→export type CommandName = (typeof COMMAND_NAMES)[number];
    88→
    89→/** Protocol events — ephemeral notifications pushed to all connected clients. */
    90→export const PROTOCOL_EVENTS = [
    91→  "session:plan-mode",
    92→  "session:error",
    93→  "session:progress",
    94→  "tool:request",
    95→  // PTY events (high-throughput)
    96→  "pty-data",
    97→  "pty-exit",
    98→  // File system events
    99→  "fs:changed",
   100→  // Git events
   101→  "git-clone-progress",
   102→  // Sidecar bidirectional RPC
   103→  "sidecar:request",
   104→] as const;
   105→export type ProtocolEvent = (typeof PROTOCOL_EVENTS)[number];
   106→
   107→// ============================================================================
   108→// Payload Schemas
   109→// ============================================================================
   110→
   111→export const WorkspaceProgressSchema = z.object({
   112→  workspaceId: z.string(),
   113→  step: z.string(),
   114→  label: z.string(),
   115→});
   116→export type WorkspaceProgressEvent = z.infer<typeof WorkspaceProgressSchema>;
   117→
   118→export const FileChangeSchema = z.object({
   119→  workspace_path: z.string(),
   120→  change_type: z.enum(["fileschanged", "metadataonly"]),
   121→  affected_count: z.number(),
   122→});
   123→export type FileChangeEvent = z.infer<typeof FileChangeSchema>;
   124→
   125→export const PtyDataSchema = z.object({
   126→  id: z.string(),
   127→  data: z.array(z.number()),
   128→});
   129→export type PtyDataEvent = z.infer<typeof PtyDataSchema>;
   130→
   131→export const PtyExitSchema = z.object({
   132→  id: z.string(),
   133→});
   134→export type PtyExitEvent = z.infer<typeof PtyExitSchema>;
   135→
   136→export const BrowserPageLoadSchema = z.object({
   137→  label: z.string(),
   138→  url: z.string(),
   139→  event: z.string(),
   140→});
   141→export type BrowserPageLoadEvent = z.infer<typeof BrowserPageLoadSchema>;
   142→
   143→export const BrowserTitleChangedSchema = z.object({
   144→  label: z.string(),
   145→  title: z.string(),
   146→});
   147→export type BrowserTitleChangedEvent = z.infer<typeof BrowserTitleChangedSchema>;
   148→
   149→export const BrowserUrlChangeSchema = z.object({
   150→  label: z.string(),
   151→  url: z.string(),
   152→});
   153→export type BrowserUrlChangeEvent = z.infer<typeof BrowserUrlChangeSchema>;
   154→
   155→export const BrowserWorkspaceChangeSchema = z.object({
   156→  workspaceId: z.string(),
   157→  directoryName: z.string().nullish(),
   158→  repoName: z.string().nullish(),
   159→  branch: z.string().nullish(),
   160→});
   161→export type BrowserWorkspaceChangeEvent = z.infer<typeof BrowserWorkspaceChangeSchema>;
   162→
   163→export const SimBuildLogSchema = z.object({
   164→  workspaceId: z.string(),
   165→  line: z.string(),
   166→});
   167→export type SimBuildLogEvent = z.infer<typeof SimBuildLogSchema>;
   168→
   169→export const BackendPortChangedSchema = z.object({
   170→  port: z.number(),
   171→});
   172→export type BackendPortChangedEvent = z.infer<typeof BackendPortChangedSchema>;
   173→
   174→export const GitCloneProgressSchema = z.object({
   175→  percent: z.number(),
   176→  received: z.number(),
   177→  total: z.number(),
   178→  received_bytes: z.number(),
   179→  status: z.string(),
   180→  phase: z.enum(["connecting", "receiving", "indexing", "resolving", "complete"]),
   181→});
   182→export type GitCloneProgressEvent = z.infer<typeof GitCloneProgressSchema>;
   183→
   184→/** InspectElement schema — matches the shape emitted by the browser InSpec handler.
   185→ *  Keep in sync with InspectElement in parseInspectTags.ts. */
   186→const InspectElementSchema = z.object({
   187→  ref: z.string(),
   188→  tagName: z.string(),
   189→  path: z.string(),
   190→  innerText: z.string().optional(),
   191→  context: z.enum(["local", "external"]).optional(),
   192→  reactComponent: z.string().optional(),
   193→  file: z.string().optional(),
   194→  line: z.string().optional(),
   195→  styles: z.string().optional(),
   196→  props: z.string().optional(),
   197→  attributes: z.string().optional(),
   198→  innerHTML: z.string().optional(),
   199→});
   200→
   201→/** Serialized chat insert payload (main process → frontend).
   202→ *  Keep in sync with SerializedChatInsertPayload in chatInsertStore.ts. */
   203→export const ChatInsertSchema = z.discriminatedUnion("type", [
   204→  z.object({
   205→    type: z.literal("text"),
   206→    workspaceId: z.string(),
   207→    text: z.string(),
   208→  }),
   209→  z.object({
   210→    type: z.literal("element"),
   211→    workspaceId: z.string(),
   212→    element: InspectElementSchema,
   213→  }),
   214→  z.object({
   215→    type: z.literal("files"),
   216→    workspaceId: z.string(),
   217→    files: z.array(
   218→      z.object({
   219→        name: z.string(),
   220→        type: z.string(),
   221→        lastModified: z.number(),
   222→        base64: z.string(),
   223→      })
   224→    ),
   225→  }),
   226→]);
   227→export type SerializedChatInsertPayload = z.infer<typeof ChatInsertSchema>;
   228→
   229→// ============================================================================
   230→// Runtime Schema Map (event name → Zod schema)
   231→// ============================================================================
   232→
   233→/**
   234→ * Maps every known event name to its Zod schema for runtime validation.
   235→ * Used by the typed `listen()` wrapper to validate payloads crossing
   236→ * the IPC boundary — catches payload drift at runtime.
   237→ */
   238→export const AppEventSchemaMap = {
   239→  [BACKEND_PORT_CHANGED]: BackendPortChangedSchema,
   240→  [WORKSPACE_PROGRESS]: WorkspaceProgressSchema,
   241→  [FS_CHANGED]: FileChangeSchema,
   242→  [PTY_DATA]: PtyDataSchema,
   243→  [PTY_EXIT]: PtyExitSchema,
   244→  [BROWSER_PAGE_LOAD]: BrowserPageLoadSchema,
   245→  [BROWSER_TITLE_CHANGED]: BrowserTitleChangedSchema,
   246→  [BROWSER_URL_CHANGE]: BrowserUrlChangeSchema,
   247→  [BROWSER_WORKSPACE_CHANGE]: BrowserWorkspaceChangeSchema,
   248→  [BROWSER_DETACHED_CLOSED]: z.undefined(),
   249→  [SIM_BUILD_LOG]: SimBuildLogSchema,
   250→  [CHAT_INSERT]: ChatInsertSchema,
   251→  [GIT_CLONE_PROGRESS]: GitCloneProgressSchema,
   252→} as const satisfies Record<AppEventName, z.ZodTypeAny>;
   253→
   254→// ============================================================================
   255→// Type-Safe Event Map
   256→// ============================================================================
   257→
   258→/**
   259→ * Maps every app event name to its payload type.
   260→ * Used by the typed `listen()` wrapper in platform/electron to provide
   261→ * autocomplete on event names and auto-inferred payload types.
   262→ */
   263→export interface AppEventMap {
   264→  // Backend lifecycle
   265→  [BACKEND_PORT_CHANGED]: BackendPortChangedEvent;
   266→
   267→  // Workspace
   268→  [WORKSPACE_PROGRESS]: WorkspaceProgressEvent;
   269→
   270→  // File system
   271→  [FS_CHANGED]: FileChangeEvent;
   272→
   273→  // PTY
   274→  [PTY_DATA]: PtyDataEvent;
   275→  [PTY_EXIT]: PtyExitEvent;
   276→
   277→  // Browser
   278→  [BROWSER_PAGE_LOAD]: BrowserPageLoadEvent;
   279→  [BROWSER_TITLE_CHANGED]: BrowserTitleChangedEvent;
   280→  [BROWSER_URL_CHANGE]: BrowserUrlChangeEvent;
   281→  [BROWSER_WORKSPACE_CHANGE]: BrowserWorkspaceChangeEvent;
   282→  [BROWSER_DETACHED_CLOSED]: undefined;
   283→
   284→  // Simulator
   285→  [SIM_BUILD_LOG]: SimBuildLogEvent;
   286→
   287→  // Chat
   288→  [CHAT_INSERT]: SerializedChatInsertPayload;
   289→
   290→  // Git
   291→  [GIT_CLONE_PROGRESS]: GitCloneProgressEvent;
   292→}
   293→
   294→/** Union of all known app event names */
   295→export type AppEventName = keyof AppEventMap;
   296→

1→/**
     2→ * Typed query functions — centralized DB reads for all route handlers.
     3→ *
     4→ * Every `as` cast lives here (once per query), not in routes.
     5→ * Route handlers call these functions instead of inline SQL.
     6→ */
     7→import type Database from "better-sqlite3";
     8→import type {
     9→  RepositoryRow,
    10→  RepositoryWithCountsRow,
    11→  WorkspaceRow,
    12→  WorkspaceWithDetailsRow,
    13→  SessionRow,
    14→  SessionWithDetailsRow,
    15→  MessageRow,
    16→  StatsRow,
    17→} from "./types";
    18→
    19→// ─── Workspace Queries ───────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Canonical workspace + repo + session JOIN.
    23→ * Single source of truth — reused by list, get-by-id, and by-repo queries.
    24→ */
    25→const WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT = `
    26→  SELECT
    27→    w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    28→    w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.current_session_id,
    29→    w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    30→    w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    31→    w.updated_at,
    32→    r.name as repo_name, r.root_path, r.git_default_branch,
    33→    s.status as session_status, s.model,
    34→    s.error_category as session_error_category,
    35→    s.error_message as session_error_message,
    36→    s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    37→  FROM workspaces w
    38→  LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    39→  LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    40→`;
    41→
    42→export function getAllWorkspaces(db: Database.Database): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    43→  return db
    44→    .prepare(
    45→      `
    46→    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    47→    ORDER BY w.updated_at DESC
    48→    LIMIT 100
    49→  `
    50→    )
    51→    .all() as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    52→}
    53→
    54→export function getWorkspacesByRepo(
    55→  db: Database.Database,
    56→  state?: string
    57→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    58→  // Support comma-separated states (e.g. "ready,initializing")
    59→  let stateFilter = "";
    60→  let stateParams: string[] = [];
    61→  if (state) {
    62→    const states = state.split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
    63→    stateFilter = `WHERE w.state IN (${states.map(() => "?").join(",")})`;
    64→    stateParams = states;
    65→  }
    66→  return db
    67→    .prepare(
    68→      `
    69→    SELECT
    70→      w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    71→      w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.current_session_id,
    72→      w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    73→      w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    74→      w.updated_at,
    75→      r.name as repo_name, r.sort_order as repo_sort_order, r.root_path,
    76→      r.git_default_branch,
    77→      s.status as session_status, s.model,
    78→      s.error_category as session_error_category,
    79→      s.error_message as session_error_message,
    80→      s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    81→    FROM workspaces w
    82→    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    83→    LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    84→    ${stateFilter}
    85→    ORDER BY r.sort_order, r.name, w.updated_at DESC
    86→  `
    87→    )
    88→    .all(...stateParams) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    89→}
    90→
    91→export function getWorkspaceById(
    92→  db: Database.Database,
    93→  id: string
    94→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined {
    95→  return db
    96→    .prepare(
    97→      `
    98→    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    99→    WHERE w.id = ?
   100→  `
   101→    )
   102→    .get(id) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined;
   103→}
   104→
   105→/** Used by withWorkspace middleware — lighter query without session JOIN. */
   106→export function getWorkspaceForMiddleware(
   107→  db: Database.Database,
   108→  id: string
   109→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined {
   110→  return db
   111→    .prepare(
   112→      `
   113→    SELECT w.*, r.root_path, r.git_default_branch, r.name as repo_name
   114→    FROM workspaces w
   115→    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
   116→    WHERE w.id = ?
   117→  `
   118→    )
   119→    .get(id) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined;
   120→}
   121→
   122→export function getWorkspaceRaw(db: Database.Database, id: string): WorkspaceRow | undefined {
   123→  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM workspaces WHERE id = ?").get(id) as WorkspaceRow | undefined;
   124→}
   125→
   126→/**
   127→ * Look up workspaces by their current session IDs.
   128→ * Used by the query engine for delta pushes — when a session status changes,
   129→ * we only need the affected workspace(s) instead of re-querying everything.
   130→ */
   131→export function getWorkspacesBySessionIds(
   132→  db: Database.Database,
   133→  sessionIds: string[]
   134→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
   135→  if (sessionIds.length === 0) return [];
   136→  const placeholders = sessionIds.map(() => "?").join(",");
   137→  return db
   138→    .prepare(
   139→      `
   140→    SELECT
   141→      w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
   142→      w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.current_session_id,
   143→      w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
   144→      w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
   145→      w.updated_at,
   146→      r.name as repo_name, r.sort_order as repo_sort_order, r.root_path,
   147→      r.git_default_branch,
   148→      s.status as session_status, s.model,
   149→      s.error_category as session_error_category,
   150→      s.error_message as session_error_message,
   151→      s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
   152→    FROM workspaces w
   153→    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
   154→    LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
   155→    WHERE w.current_session_id IN (${placeholders})
   156→  `
   157→    )
   158→    .all(...sessionIds) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
   159→}
   160→
   161→/**
   162→ * Non-archived workspaces for query engine and relay clients.
   163→ * Used by: query-engine snapshots, relay initial state, relay data requests.
   164→ */
   165→export function getDashboardWorkspaces(db: Database.Database): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
   166→  return db
   167→    .prepare(
   168→      `
   169→    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
   170→    WHERE w.state != 'archived'
   171→    ORDER BY r.sort_order ASC, r.name ASC, w.updated_at DESC
   172→  `
   173→    )
   174→    .all() as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
   175→}
   176→
   177→// ─── Session Queries ─────────────────────────────────────────
   178→
   179→/** Coerce SQLite INTEGER booleans (0/1) to JS booleans so HTTP and IPC return the same shape. */
   180→function coerceSessionBooleans<T extends SessionRow>(row: T): T {
   181→  return { ...row, is_hidden: Boolean(row.is_hidden) };
   182→}
   183→
   184→/**
   185→ * Session query — uses denormalized message_count column
   186→ * instead of COUNT(m.id) JOIN, eliminating expensive aggregation.
   187→ */
   188→const SESSION_DETAILS_SELECT = `
   189→  SELECT s.*, w.slug, w.state as workspace_state
   190→  FROM sessions s
   191→  LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON s.id = w.current_session_id
   192→`;
   193→
   194→export function getAllSessions(db: Database.Database): SessionWithDetailsRow[] {
   195→  const rows = db
   196→    .prepare(
   197→      `
   198→    ${SESSION_DETAILS_SELECT}
   199→    ORDER BY s.updated_at DESC
   200→    LIMIT 50
   201→  `
   202→    )
   203→    .all() as SessionWithDetailsRow[];
   204→  return rows.map(coerceSessionBooleans);
   205→}
   206→
   207→export function getSessionById(
   208→  db: Database.Database,
   209→  id: string
   210→): SessionWithDetailsRow | undefined {
   211→  const row = db
   212→    .prepare(
   213→      `
   214→    ${SESSION_DETAILS_SELECT}
   215→    WHERE s.id = ?
   216→  `
   217→    )
   218→    .get(id) as SessionWithDetailsRow | undefined;
   219→  return row ? coerceSessionBooleans(row) : undefined;
   220→}
   221→
   222→export function getSessionRaw(db: Database.Database, id: string): SessionRow | undefined {
   223→  const row = db.prepare("SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?").get(id) as SessionRow | undefined;
   224→  return row ? coerceSessionBooleans(row) : undefined;
   225→}
   226→
   227→/** All sessions for a workspace, ordered by creation time (UUID7 is chronological). */
   228→export function getSessionsByWorkspaceId(db: Database.Database, workspaceId: string): SessionRow[] {
   229→  const rows = db
   230→    .prepare("SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE workspace_id = ? ORDER BY id ASC")
   231→    .all(workspaceId) as SessionRow[];
   232→  return rows.map(coerceSessionBooleans);
   233→}
   234→
   235→// ─── Message Queries ─────────────────────────────────────────
   236→
   237→/**
   238→ * Fetch paginated messages for a session.
   239→ * Uses seq (per-session monotonic integer) as cursor — no collisions,
   240→ * no NULLs, integer comparison. sent_at had 15K+ collisions in production.
   241→ */
   242→export function getMessages(
   243→  db: Database.Database,
   244→  sessionId: string,
   245→  opts: { limit: number; before?: number; after?: number }
   246→): MessageRow[] {
   247→  if (opts.before) {
   248→    // Load older: fetch N messages before seq, then re-sort ascending
   249→    return db
   250→      .prepare(
   251→        `
   252→      SELECT * FROM (
   253→        SELECT * FROM messages
   254→        WHERE session_id = ? AND seq < ?
   255→        ORDER BY seq DESC
   256→        LIMIT ?
   257→      ) sub ORDER BY seq ASC
   258→    `
   259→      )
   260→      .all(sessionId, opts.before, opts.limit) as MessageRow[];
   261→  }
   262→
   263→  if (opts.after) {
   264→    // Load newer: fetch N messages after seq, already in ascending order
   265→    return db
   266→      .prepare(
   267→        `
   268→      SELECT * FROM messages
   269→      WHERE session_id = ? AND seq > ?
   270→      ORDER BY seq ASC
   271→      LIMIT ?
   272→    `
   273→      )
   274→      .all(sessionId, opts.after, opts.limit) as MessageRow[];
   275→  }
   276→
   277→  // Default: load latest N messages (DESC then re-wrap ASC)
   278→  return db
   279→    .prepare(
   280→      `
   281→    SELECT * FROM (
   282→      SELECT * FROM messages
   283→      WHERE session_id = ?
   284→      ORDER BY seq DESC
   285→      LIMIT ?
   286→    ) sub ORDER BY seq ASC
   287→  `
   288→    )
   289→    .all(sessionId, opts.limit) as MessageRow[];
   290→}
   291→
   292→export function hasOlderMessages(db: Database.Database, sessionId: string, seq: number): boolean {
   293→  return !!db
   294→    .prepare("SELECT 1 FROM messages WHERE session_id = ? AND seq < ? LIMIT 1")
   295→    .get(sessionId, seq);
   296→}
   297→
   298→export function hasNewerMessages(db: Database.Database, sessionId: string, seq: number): boolean {
   299→  return !!db
   300→    .prepare("SELECT 1 FROM messages WHERE session_id = ? AND seq > ? LIMIT 1")
   301→    .get(sessionId, seq);
   302→}
   303→
   304→export function getLastAssistantAgentMessageId(
   305→  db: Database.Database,
   306→  sessionId: string
   307→): string | null {
   308→  const row = db
   309→    .prepare(
   310→      `
   311→    SELECT agent_message_id FROM messages
   312→    WHERE session_id = ? AND role = 'assistant' AND agent_message_id IS NOT NULL
   313→    ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1
   314→  `
   315→    )
   316→    .get(sessionId) as { agent_message_id: string } | undefined;
   317→  return row?.agent_message_id ?? null;
   318→}
   319→
   320→export function getMessageById(db: Database.Database, id: string): MessageRow | undefined {
   321→  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM messages WHERE id = ?").get(id) as MessageRow | undefined;
   322→}
   323→
   324→/** Get the highest seq for a session (cursor initialization for real-time streaming). */
   325→export function getMaxMessageSeq(db: Database.Database, sessionId: string): number {
   326→  const row = db
   327→    .prepare("SELECT COALESCE(MAX(seq), 0) as max_seq FROM messages WHERE session_id = ?")
   328→    .get(sessionId) as { max_seq: number } | undefined;
   329→  return row?.max_seq ?? 0;
   330→}
   331→
   332→/** Fetch all messages after a given seq (delta push for real-time streaming). */
   333→export function getMessagesDelta(
   334→  db: Database.Database,
   335→  sessionId: string,
   336→  afterSeq: number
   337→): MessageRow[] {
   338→  return db
   339→    .prepare("SELECT * FROM messages WHERE session_id = ? AND seq > ? ORDER BY seq ASC")
   340→    .all(sessionId, afterSeq) as MessageRow[];
   341→}
   342→
   343→// ─── Repository Queries ─────────────────────────────────────
   344→
   345→export function getAllRepositories(db: Database.Database): RepositoryWithCountsRow[] {
   346→  return db
   347→    .prepare(
   348→      `
   349→    SELECT r.*,
   350→           COUNT(CASE WHEN w.state = 'ready' THEN 1 END) as ready_count,
   351→           COUNT(CASE WHEN w.state = 'archived' THEN 1 END) as archived_count,
   352→           COUNT(w.id) as total_count
   353→    FROM repositories r
   354→    LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON w.repository_id = r.id
   355→    GROUP BY r.id
   356→    ORDER BY r.sort_order, r.name
   357→  `
   358→    )
   359→    .all() as RepositoryWithCountsRow[];
   360→}
   361→
   362→/** Lightweight repo list for backfilling empty groups in by-repo queries. */
   363→export function getAllRepositorySummaries(
   364→  db: Database.Database
   365→): { id: string; name: string; sort_order: number }[] {
   366→  return db
   367→    .prepare("SELECT id, name, sort_order FROM repositories ORDER BY sort_order, name")
   368→    .all() as { id: string; name: string; sort_order: number }[];
   369→}
   370→
   371→export function getRepositoryById(db: Database.Database, id: string): RepositoryRow | undefined {
   372→  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM repositories WHERE id = ?").get(id) as RepositoryRow | undefined;
   373→}
   374→
   375→export function getRepositoryByRootPath(
   376→  db: Database.Database,
   377→  rootPath: string
   378→): RepositoryRow | undefined {
   379→  return db.prepare("SELECT * FROM repositories WHERE root_path = ?").get(rootPath) as
   380→    | RepositoryRow
   381→    | undefined;
   382→}
   383→
   384→export function getMaxRepositorySortOrder(db: Database.Database): number {
   385→  const row = db.prepare("SELECT MAX(sort_order) as max FROM repositories").get() as {
   386→    max: number | null;
   387→  };
   388→  return row?.max ?? 0;
   389→}
   390→
   391→// ─── Stats Queries ───────────────────────────────────────────
   392→
   393→let cachedStats: StatsRow | null = null;
   394→let cachedAt = 0;
   395→const STATS_TTL_MS = 5_000;
   396→
   397→export function getStats(db: Database.Database): StatsRow {
   398→  const now = Date.now();
   399→  if (cachedStats && now - cachedAt < STATS_TTL_MS) return cachedStats;
   400→  cachedStats = db
   401→    .prepare(
   402→      `
   403→    SELECT
   404→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces) as workspaces,
   405→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE state = 'ready') as workspaces_ready,
   406→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workspaces WHERE state = 'archived') as workspaces_archived,
   407→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM repositories) as repositories,
   408→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions) as sessions,
   409→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE status = 'idle') as sessions_idle,
   410→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE status = 'working') as sessions_working,
   411→      (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM messages) as messages
   412→  `
   413→    )
   414→    .get() as StatsRow;
   415→  cachedAt = now;
   416→  return cachedStats;
   417→}
   418→
   419→/** Reset the in-memory stats cache (for testing). */
   420→export function resetStatsCache(): void {
   421→  cachedStats = null;
   422→  cachedAt = 0;
   423→}
   424→

1→import { z } from "zod";
     2→import { WorkspaceStateSchema } from "@shared/enums";
     3→import { ValidationError } from "./errors";
     4→
     5→// ============================================================================
     6→// Config Schemas
     7→// ============================================================================
     8→
     9→export const McpServerSchema = z.object({
    10→  name: z.string().min(1),
    11→  command: z.string().min(1),
    12→  args: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    13→  env: z.record(z.string(), z.string()).optional(),
    14→  enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
    15→});
    16→
    17→export const SaveMcpServersBody = z.object({
    18→  servers: z.array(McpServerSchema),
    19→});
    20→
    21→export const SaveCommandBody = z.object({
    22→  name: z.string().min(1, "name is required"),
    23→  content: z.string().min(1, "content is required"),
    24→});
    25→
    26→export const SaveAgentBody = z.object({
    27→  id: z.string().min(1, "id is required"),
    28→  name: z.string().optional(),
    29→  description: z.string().optional(),
    30→  tools: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    31→});
    32→
    33→const HookCommand = z
    34→  .object({
    35→    type: z.string().optional(),
    36→    command: z.string(),
    37→    timeout: z.number().optional(),
    38→  })
    39→  .passthrough();
    40→
    41→const HookMatcherGroup = z
    42→  .object({
    43→    matcher: z.string().optional(),
    44→    hooks: z.array(HookCommand).optional(),
    45→  })
    46→  .passthrough();
    47→
    48→export const SaveHooksBody = z.object({
    49→  hooks: z.record(z.string(), z.array(HookMatcherGroup)),
    50→});
    51→
    52→export const SaveSkillBody = z.object({
    53→  name: z.string().min(1, "name is required"),
    54→  content: z.string().min(1, "content is required"),
    55→});
    56→
    57→// ============================================================================
    58→// Session Schemas
    59→// ============================================================================
    60→
    61→export const CreateMessageBody = z.object({
    62→  content: z.string().min(1, "content is required"),
    63→  model: z.string().optional(),
    64→});
    65→
    66→// ============================================================================
    67→// Repo Schemas
    68→// ============================================================================
    69→
    70→export const CreateRepoBody = z.object({
    71→  root_path: z.string().min(1, "root_path is required"),
    72→});
    73→
    74→// ============================================================================
    75→// Workspace Schemas

    77→
    78→// WorkspaceStateSchema imported from shared/enums.ts — single source of truth.
    79→// Previously had a local 5-value enum including "working" which doesn't belong

    82→export const PatchWorkspaceBody = z.object({

    88→});
    89→
    90→export const OpenPenFileBody = z.object({
    91→  filePath: z.string().min(1, "filePath is required"),
    92→});
    93→
    94→// ============================================================================
    95→// Config File Schemas (disk reads — used with safeParse for graceful fallback)
    96→// ============================================================================
    97→
    98→/** Shape of a single MCP server entry in ~/.claude/plugins/config.json */
    99→const McpServerConfigEntry = z
   100→  .object({

   178→export function parseBody<T extends z.ZodType>(schema: T, data: unknown): z.infer<T> {
   179→  const result = schema.safeParse(data);
   180→  if (!result.success) {

   182→      const path = i.path.length > 0 ? `${i.path.join(".")}: ` : "";
   183→      return `${path}${i.message}`;

toolUseResult.originalFile:
/**
 * OpenDevs Database Schema — Single source of truth.
 *
 * Imported by backend/src/lib/schema.ts.
 * All statements are idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS).
 *
 * Tables: repositories, workspaces, sessions, messages, paired_devices
 * Indexes: 10
 * Triggers: 5 (3 auto-update updated_at, 2 denormalized message_count + auto-seq)
 */

/**
 * Post-launch migrations.
 * Each statement is an ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN that may already exist.
 * Run each individually — catch "duplicate column" errors and skip.
 * Runs AFTER SCHEMA_SQL so tables already exist.
 *
 * Currently empty — SCHEMA_SQL defines the full schema for fresh installs.
 * Add ALTER TABLE statements here when the schema changes post-launch.
 */
export const MIGRATIONS: string[] = [
  // sessions: structured error category for category-aware UI
  `ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN error_category TEXT`,
];

export const SCHEMA_SQL = `
  -- Repositories tracked by the app (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS repositories (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    root_path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    git_default_branch TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'main',
    sort_order INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    git_origin_url TEXT,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Git worktrees tied to repositories (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS workspaces (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    repository_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES repositories(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    slug TEXT NOT NULL,
    title TEXT,
    git_branch TEXT,
    git_target_branch TEXT,
    state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'initializing',
    current_session_id TEXT,
    pr_url TEXT,
    pr_number INTEGER,
    archive_commit TEXT,
    archived_at TEXT,
    setup_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'none',
    init_stage TEXT,
    error_message TEXT,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Agent sessions tied to workspaces (id = UUID7, embeds created_at)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sessions (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    workspace_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES workspaces(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    agent_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'claude',
    model TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'opus',
    agent_session_id TEXT,
    title TEXT,
    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle',
    message_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    error_message TEXT,
    error_category TEXT,
    last_user_message_at TEXT,
    context_token_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    context_used_percent REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    is_hidden INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Chat messages within sessions (id = UUID7, embeds created_at; append-only, no updated_at)
  -- seq is a per-session monotonic integer for reliable cursor pagination.
  -- Auto-assigned by trigger — never set manually in INSERT.
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS messages (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    session_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    seq INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    role TEXT NOT NULL,
    content TEXT,
    turn_id TEXT,
    model TEXT,
    agent_message_id TEXT,
    sent_at TEXT,
    cancelled_at TEXT,
    parent_tool_use_id TEXT
  );

  -- Paired devices for remote access authentication
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS paired_devices (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
    name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'Unknown Device',
    token_hash TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    ip_address TEXT,
    user_agent TEXT,
    last_seen_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );

  -- Indexes (10)
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_repository_id ON workspaces(repository_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_workspaces_state ON workspaces(state);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_workspace_id ON sessions(workspace_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_sessions_status ON sessions(status);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_seq ON messages(session_id, seq DESC);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_sent_at ON messages(session_id, sent_at);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_session_role ON messages(session_id, role, id DESC);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_turn_id ON messages(session_id, turn_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_messages_parent_tool_use ON messages(parent_tool_use_id);
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_paired_devices_token_hash ON paired_devices(token_hash);

  -- Triggers: auto-update updated_at (3)
  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_repositories_updated_at
    AFTER UPDATE ON repositories
    BEGIN UPDATE repositories SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id; END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_workspaces_updated_at
    AFTER UPDATE ON workspaces
    BEGIN UPDATE workspaces SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id; END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS update_sessions_updated_at
    AFTER UPDATE ON sessions
    BEGIN UPDATE sessions SET updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = NEW.id; END;

  -- Triggers: denormalized message_count + auto-seq on messages (2)
  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS assign_message_seq
    AFTER INSERT ON messages
    BEGIN
      UPDATE messages
        SET seq = (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(m.seq), 0) + 1 FROM messages m WHERE m.session_id = NEW.session_id AND m.id != NEW.id)
        WHERE id = NEW.id;
      UPDATE sessions SET message_count = message_count + 1 WHERE id = NEW.session_id;
    END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS dec_session_message_count
    AFTER DELETE ON messages
    BEGIN UPDATE sessions SET message_count = message_count - 1 WHERE id = OLD.session_id; END;
`;

        SET seq = (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(m.seq), 0) + 1 FROM messages m WHERE m.session_id = NEW.session_id AND m.id != NEW.id)
        WHERE id = NEW.id;
      UPDATE sessions SET message_count = message_count + 1 WHERE id = NEW.session_id;
    END;

  CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS dec_session_message_count
    AFTER DELETE ON messages
    BEGIN UPDATE sessions SET message_count = message_count - 1 WHERE id = OLD.session_id; END;
`;

content.text:
Pre-existing integration test failures (better-sqlite3 compiled against different Node.js version). Not related to our changes. All 390 unit tests pass.

Let me do a quick summary of all changes.

**All 390 backend tests pass. Type check clean. Here's what was implemented:**

## Changes Summary (17 files, 1 new)

### Shared layer
1. **`shared/enums.ts`** — Added `WorkspaceStatusSchema`, `STICKY_STATUSES`, `STATUS_RANK`
2. **`shared/types/workspace.ts`** — Added `status: WorkspaceStatus` to `Workspace` interface
3. **`shared/schema.ts`** — Added `status` column to CREATE TABLE, migration entry, and index
4. **`shared/events.ts`** — Added `"updateWorkspaceStatus"` to `MUTATION_NAMES`

### Backend
5. **`apps/backend/src/db/types.ts`** — Added `status: string` to `WorkspaceRow`, `WorkspaceWithDetailsRow`, and status counts to `StatsRow`
6. **`apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts`** — Added `w.status` to all 4 workspace SELECT queries, status count stats, and `backfillWorkspaceStatus()`
7. **`apps/backend/src/lib/schemas.ts`** — Added `status: WorkspaceStatusSchema.optional()` to `PatchWorkspaceBody`
8. **`apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts`** — Call `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` after migrations
9. **`apps/backend/src/services/workspace-status.service.ts`** *(NEW)* — `autoProgressStatus()` with sticky/flow/rank guards + `setWorkspaceStatus()`
10. **`apps/backend/src/routes/workspaces.ts`** — PATCH handler: manual status override, archive→done, unarchive→in-progress
11. **`apps/backend/src/routes/workspaces.pr.ts`** — Persist `pr_url` on discovery → auto-derive `in-review`; merge → `done`
12. **`apps/backend/src/services/query-engine.ts`** — Added `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation + `autoProgressStatus` on archive mutation

### Frontend
13. **`apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts`** — Added `WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` (labels + colors for all 5 statuses)
14. **`apps/web/src/features/workspace/api/workspace.service.ts`** — Added `updateStatus()` method
15. **`apps/web/src/features/workspace/api/workspace.queries.ts`** — Added `useUpdateWorkspaceStatus()` mutation hook with optimistic update
16. **`apps/web/src/features/workspace/types.ts`** — Re-exported `WorkspaceStatus`
17. **`apps/web/src/features/workspace/lib/workspace.utils.ts`** — Added `status: "in-progress"` to optimistic workspace

### Test/Storybook fixes
- **`apps/backend/test/unit/shared/events.test.ts`** — Updated mutation count assertion
- **`apps/web/src/features/sidebar/ui/WorkspaceItem.stories.tsx`** — Added `status` to mock
- **`apps/web/src/platform/analytics/events.ts`** — Added `workspace_status_changed` event

The UI rendering (status icon + dropdown in WorkspaceItem) is not included — that's a separate design pass as noted in the spec. All the data plumbing is wired and ready for it.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/zvada/.claude/skills/code-simplifier

<!--
Based on Anthropic's code-simplifier agent:
https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/blob/main/plugins/code-simplifier/agents/code-simplifier.md
-->

# Code Simplifier

You are an expert code simplification specialist focused on enhancing code clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving exact functionality. Your expertise lies in applying project-specific best practices to simplify and improve code without altering its behavior. You prioritize readable, explicit code over overly compact solutions.

## Refinement Principles

### 1. Preserve Functionality

Never change what the code does - only how it does it. All original features, outputs, and behaviors must remain intact.

### 2. Apply Project Standards

Follow the established coding standards from CLAUDE.md including:

- Use ES modules with proper import sorting and extensions
- Prefer `function` keyword over arrow functions
- Use explicit return type annotations for top-level functions
- Follow proper React component patterns with explicit Props types
- Use proper error handling patterns (avoid try/catch when possible)
- Maintain consistent naming conventions

### 3. Enhance Clarity

Simplify code structure by:

- Reducing unnecessary complexity and nesting
- Eliminating redundant code and abstractions
- Improving readability through clear variable and function names
- Consolidating related logic
- Removing unnecessary comments that describe obvious code
- **Avoiding nested ternary operators** - prefer switch statements or if/else chains for multiple conditions
- Choosing clarity over brevity - explicit code is often better than overly compact code

### 4. Maintain Balance

Avoid over-simplification that could:

- Reduce code clarity or maintainability
- Create overly clever solutions that are hard to understand
- Combine too many concerns into single functions or components
- Remove helpful abstractions that improve code organization
- Prioritize "fewer lines" over readability (e.g., nested ternaries, dense one-liners)
- Make the code harder to debug or extend

### 5. Focus Scope

Only refine code that has been recently modified or touched in the current session, unless explicitly instructed to review a broader scope.

## Refinement Process

1. **Identify** the recently modified code sections
2. **Analyze** for opportunities to improve elegance and consistency
3. **Apply** project-specific best practices and coding standards
4. **Ensure** all functionality remains unchanged
5. **Verify** the refined code is simpler and more maintainable
6. **Document** only significant changes that affect understanding

## Examples

### Before: Nested Ternaries

```typescript
const status = isLoading ? 'loading' : hasError ? 'error' : isComplete ? 'complete' : 'idle';
```

### After: Clear Switch Statement

```typescript
function getStatus(isLoading: boolean, hasError: boolean, isComplete: boolean): string {
  if (isLoading) return 'loading';
  if (hasError) return 'error';
  if (isComplete) return 'complete';
  return 'idle';
}
```

### Before: Overly Compact

```typescript
const result = arr.filter(x => x > 0).map(x => x * 2).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
```

### After: Clear Steps

```typescript
const positiveNumbers = arr.filter(x => x > 0);
const doubled = positiveNumbers.map(x => x * 2);
const sum = doubled.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
```

### Before: Redundant Abstraction

```typescript
function isNotEmpty(arr: unknown[]): boolean {
  return arr.length > 0;
}

if (isNotEmpty(items)) {
  // ...
}
```

### After: Direct Check

```typescript
if (items.length > 0) {
  // ...
}
```

import type { Workspace } from "@/features/workspace/types";
import type { WorkspaceStatus } from "@shared/enums";

/**
 * Display status extends SessionStatus with derived states.
 * 'unread' is derived from needs_response / needs_plan_response session status.
 */
export type DisplayStatus = "idle" | "working" | "error" | "unread";

/**
 * Priority levels for sorting (higher = more urgent)
 */
export enum StatusPriority {
  ERROR = 4, // Critical - something broke
  UNREAD = 3, // Important - needs review / response
  WORKING = 2, // Active - in progress
  IDLE = 0, // Dormant - no activity
}

/**
 * Visual configuration for each status
 *
 * Design System:
 * - badge: Color for notification badges (collapsed sidebar)
 * - border: Border/glow color for active states
 * - text: Text color for status labels
 * - bg: Background color for sections/cards
 *
 * Color Semantics:
 * 🔴 Red (error) = Critical, blocking, needs immediate action

    28→/** Workspace lifecycle states (git worktree). */
    29→export const WorkspaceStateSchema = z.enum(["ready", "initializing", "archived", "error"]);
    30→export type WorkspaceState = z.infer<typeof WorkspaceStateSchema>;
    31→
    32→/** Workspace initialization progress. */
    33→export const SetupStatusSchema = z.enum(["none", "running", "completed", "failed"]);
    34→export type SetupStatus = z.infer<typeof SetupStatusSchema>;
    35→
    36→/** Linear-style workflow states for workspaces. */
    37→export const WorkspaceStatusSchema = z.enum([
    38→  "backlog",
    39→  "in-progress",
    40→  "in-review",
    41→  "done",
    42→  "canceled",
    43→]);
    44→export type WorkspaceStatus = z.infer<typeof WorkspaceStatusSchema>;
    45→
    46→/** Sticky states resist auto-progression. Only user action (or archive) can exit. */
    47→export const STICKY_STATUSES: ReadonlySet<WorkspaceStatus> = new Set(["backlog", "canceled"]);
    48→
    49→/** Numeric rank for progression comparison. canceled is -1 (side-exit, not in flow). */
    50→export const STATUS_RANK: Record<WorkspaceStatus, number> = {
    51→  backlog: 0,
    52→  "in-progress": 1,
    53→  "in-review": 2,
    54→  done: 3,
    55→  canceled: -1,
    56→};
    57→
    58→// ── Agent ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    59→
    60→export const AgentTypeSchema = z.enum(["claude", "codex"]);

I run AI code review, is it actionable? Wdyt? Explore in depth each problem.

We want a clean, maintainable codebase, with proper best practices for the tech stack we use.

Run subagents to verify, and if it should be fixed, try to merge some stuff together so it can be fixed together within one subagent. 

If you can test something do it.

-----


The inline comment tool (`DiffComment`) is not available in this environment, so I'll present the review findings directly.

---

## Code Review: Workspace Workflow Status

**Branch:** `zvadaadam/workspace-status`
**Summary:** Adds a `status` field to workspaces (`backlog` → `in-progress` → `in-review` → `done` / `canceled`) with auto-progression logic, backfill migration, and frontend optimistic updates.

---

### Validated Issues

### **#1 Backfill runs on every app startup and overrides user-set status**

`backfillWorkspaceStatus()` is called from `initDatabase()` on every startup with no migration guard or "already ran" flag. It updates workspaces matching `status = 'in-progress'` based on heuristics (e.g., `pr_url IS NOT NULL → in-review`). Once users can explicitly set status, restarting the app silently overrides their choice. For example: user sets a workspace with a PR back to `in-progress` → next restart pushes it to `in-review` again. The `autoProgressStatus()` function has proper sticky/rank guards, but the backfill bypasses all of them via raw SQL.

**Fix:** Add a migration guard (e.g., a one-time flag in a metadata table, or move this into a versioned migration) so it only runs once.

**File:** `apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts` (backfillWorkspaceStatus) / `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts:53`

---

### **#2 Unvalidated `status` value in WebSocket mutation allows arbitrary strings in DB**

The `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation in `query-engine.ts` reads `status` via `readStringParam` and casts with `as WorkspaceStatus` (compile-time only). No runtime Zod validation. The HTTP PATCH route validates via `PatchWorkspaceBody` → `WorkspaceStatusSchema`, but the WebSocket `q:mutate` path does not. Any arbitrary string sent via WebSocket would be written directly to the `status` column, causing: (1) the workspace to become invisible in stats counts, (2) `STATUS_RANK[current]` returning `undefined`, making `<=` comparisons always `false` and bypassing the regression guard.

**Fix:** Add `WorkspaceStatusSchema.parse(status)` before calling `setWorkspaceStatus`.

**File:** `apps/backend/src/services/query-engine.ts` (lines ~493-502)

---

### **#3 Hardcoded Tailwind color classes instead of semantic CSS variable tokens**

`WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` uses `text-amber-500`, `text-purple-500`, and `text-green-500` — raw Tailwind palette utilities. The existing `STATUS_CONFIG` in the same file consistently uses semantic tokens (`text-destructive`, `text-status-unread`, `text-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`). CLAUDE.md explicitly states: *"Don't hardcode colors: `text-green-500 dark:text-green-400` — Create semantic variables: `--color-status-success` → `text-[color:var(--status-success)]`"*

**Fix:** Define CSS variables in `global.css` (e.g., `--status-in-progress`, `--status-in-review`, `--status-done`) and reference them here.

**File:** `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` (lines ~113-119)

---

### Filtered Out (not flagged)

- **Stats COUNT consolidation** — pre-existing pattern; new code follows the same structure. Not a new violation.
- **Missing workspace existence check** — pedantic consistency issue with zero real-world impact (frontend always sends valid IDs; SQLite UPDATE on missing row is a harmless no-op).

**File:** `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` (lines ~113-119)

---

### Filtered Out (not flagged)

- **Stats COUNT consolidation** — pre-existing pattern; new code follows the same structure. Not a new violation.
- **Missing workspace existence check** — pedantic consistency issue with zero real-world impact (frontend always sends valid IDs; SQLite UPDATE on missing row is a harmless no-op).

Now I have a complete picture. Let me write up the findings:

## Investigation Results

I've traced through the code and confirmed **the claim is accurate: `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` is a real problem**. Here are the findings:

### 1. The `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` Function (lines 432-448 in `apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts`)

The function has three UPDATE statements. The problematic one is:

```sql
UPDATE workspaces SET status = 'in-review'
WHERE pr_url IS NOT NULL AND state = 'ready' AND status = 'in-progress'
```

**The WHERE clause matches exactly the scenario you described:** A workspace with a PR URL, in "ready" state, and status set to "in-progress" by the user.

### 2. YES, User Manual Override Gets Lost

Here's the exact flow:

1. User has a workspace with `pr_url = "https://..."`, `state = 'ready'`
2. User manually calls the PATCH endpoint and sets `status = 'in-progress'` (via `setWorkspaceStatus()` in `workspace-status.service.ts` line 41-43)
3. App is restarted
4. `initDatabase()` runs (line 17-64 in `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts`)
5. Line 53 calls `backfillWorkspaceStatus(dbInstance)` unconditionally
6. The UPDATE query on line 444-447 matches the user's workspace and overwrites `status` back to `'in-review'`

**Result:** User's manual `in-progress` status is silently overwritten.

### 3. No "Already Ran" Detection in Migration System

The MIGRATIONS array (in `shared/schema.ts` lines 21-26) does NOT have "already ran" tracking:

```typescript
export const MIGRATIONS: string[] = [
  `ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN error_category TEXT`,
  `ALTER TABLE workspaces ADD COLUMN status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'in-progress'`,
];
```

The migration system in `database.ts` (lines 42-49) only catches SQL errors:

```typescript
for (const sql of MIGRATIONS) {
  try {
    dbInstance.exec(sql);
  } catch (e: unknown) {
    const msg = e instanceof Error ? e.message : "";
    if (!msg.includes("duplicate column") && !msg.includes("no such table")) throw e;
  }
}
```

It relies on **error detection** (`duplicate column`), not **version tracking**. There's no separate migrations table, version number, or idempotency guard to prevent `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` from running multiple times.

### 4. Best Fix Pattern: Move Backfill into the MIGRATIONS Array

The cleanest solution that matches existing patterns would be:

1. **Create a migration entry** that wraps the backfill logic
2. **Track it in a migrations table** or use a sentinel column approach

However, looking at the MIGRATIONS system's design, there are two practical approaches:

**Option A (Cleanest):** Add a `backfill_version` column to the database and check it before running backfill:
- Set `backfill_version = 1` on first successful backfill
- Only run backfill if `backfill_version IS NULL`
- This is similar to how production databases handle one-time migrations

**Option B (Simpler):** Move the backfill into a MIGRATION statement that runs once:
- Add to MIGRATIONS array: a batch SQL statement that (a) backfills statuses where still at default, (b) sets a marker
- Relies on the fact that MIGRATIONS run once per schema version, catching duplicate column errors

The current design **assumes `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` is idempotent**, with its comment on line 429 claiming "Idempotent — only updates rows still at the 'in-progress' default." But this is **false for user overrides**: if a user manually sets status to `in-progress` after the initial backfill, the next restart will override it.

### Summary

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| **Exact WHERE clauses?** | Lines 434-446. Three UPDATEs: archived with commits→done, archived without→canceled, has PR→in-review. The last one matches user overrides. |
| **Does it override user `in-progress` on PR workspace?** | YES. The condition `pr_url IS NOT NULL AND state = 'ready' AND status = 'in-progress'` matches exactly that scenario. |
| **Precedent for migration guards?** | NO. MIGRATIONS array uses error-catching only (duplicate column), not version tracking. No one-time-run mechanism. |
| **Best fix?** | Add a migration guard (either a sentinel column or version tracker) to ensure backfill runs exactly once, not every startup. |

## Investigation Summary: Hardcoded Colors in WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG

The claim is **valid** — the hardcoded Tailwind palette colors in `WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` do violate the project's documented style guidelines. However, it's **low priority** because it's an isolated outlier affecting only 3 colors in one file, and the fix is trivial.

### Key Findings

**1. Pattern Inconsistency**
- `STATUS_CONFIG` (lines 61–102) uses **semantic tokens consistently**: `text-destructive`, `text-status-unread`, `text-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`
- `WORKFLOW_STATUS_CONFIG` (lines 113–119) mixes patterns: 3 hardcoded palette colors (`text-amber-500`, `text-purple-500`, `text-green-500`) and 2 semantic tokens
- The hardcoded colors are **outliers** — only 3 instances exist in the entire 658+ lines of token-based color usage across the codebase

**2. CSS Variable Infrastructure Exists**
The project already defines semantic tokens that could be reused:
- `--status-unread` = oklch(0.53 0.14 75) ← amber-like (currently for "unread" status)
- `--status-working` = oklch(0.53 0.16 155) ← green-like (currently for "working" status)
- `--status-compacting` = oklch(0.66 0.11 345) ← purple-like (currently for "compacting" status)

However, **no workflow-specific variables exist** (`--status-in-progress`, `--status-in-review`, `--status-done`), which is why the code defaulted to hardcoded colors.

**3. Explicit Project Philosophy**
From `CLAUDE.md` styling guidelines:
> "Always use font and color tokens from the Tailwind config"
> "Never hardcode colors at all costs"

The hardcoded colors directly violate this documented principle.

**4. Impact Assessment**
- **Scope**: Tiny. Only 3 color strings across 2 workflow states in 1 file.
- **Functionality**: Zero impact. The app works perfectly.
- **Future maintainability**: Minor risk — hardcoded colors don't adapt to theme switches (light/dark mode).
- **Code quality**: Inconsistent with project standards and STATUS_CONFIG pattern.

### What the Fix Would Look Like

**In `apps/web/src/global.css` (inside `@theme` block, around line 52-57):**
```css
--color-status-in-progress: var(--warning);    /* amber */
--color-status-in-review: var(--primary);      /* purple */
--color-status-done: var(--success);           /* green */
```

**In `apps/web/src/features/sidebar/lib/status.ts` (lines 115-117):**
```ts
"in-progress": { label: "In Progress", color: "text-status-in-progress" },
"in-review":   { label: "In Review",   color: "text-status-in-review" },
"done":        { label: "Done",        color: "text-status-done" },
```

**Effort**: 2–3 minutes total (3 lines added to CSS, 3 lines updated in TypeScript).

### Recommendation

**Fix it** because it violates documented standards and is trivial to correct. However, this is **not urgent** — it doesn't block any features or break functionality. Prioritize it after higher-impact work.

Fix two issues in the workspace status feature. Working directory: /Users/zvada/Developer/agent/box-ide/.conductor/da-nang-v3

## Issue #1: Backfill runs on every startup, overrides user-set status

`backfillWorkspaceStatus()` in `apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts` runs on every app startup via `initDatabase()` in `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts`. The SQL `WHERE pr_url IS NOT NULL AND state = 'ready' AND status = 'in-progress'` will match workspaces where a user manually set status back to `in-progress` — overriding their choice.

**Fix:** Remove the `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` call from `initDatabase()` and instead add the backfill SQL statements directly into the `MIGRATIONS` array in `shared/schema.ts`. The MIGRATIONS system already handles idempotency via error catching (`duplicate column` etc). For UPDATE statements that can't fail on re-run, wrap the three backfill UPDATEs into a single compound migration that first checks whether the migration has already run (e.g., by checking if any workspace has a non-default status).

Actually, the simplest and cleanest approach: add the backfill as individual UPDATE statements in the MIGRATIONS array right after the ALTER TABLE that adds the status column. These UPDATEs are naturally one-time because:
- They only run when the ALTER TABLE preceding them succeeds (i.e., when the column is first added)
- On subsequent startups, the ALTER TABLE fails with "duplicate column" and the entire migration loop catches and continues

Wait, looking at the code more carefully: the MIGRATIONS array is a flat list, and each statement runs independently with its own try/catch. So adding backfill UPDATEs after the ALTER TABLE won't make them conditional on the ALTER TABLE.

**Better approach:** Remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` entirely. Instead, use a single compound SQL statement in MIGRATIONS that creates a temp table as a guard:

Actually the simplest: just remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` from database.ts and from queries.ts. Remove the import too. The backfill was nice for initial migration but it's dangerous to run repeatedly. Users with existing databases already had their workspaces set correctly when the ALTER TABLE ran (all defaulted to 'in-progress'). The auto-derive logic (`autoProgressStatus` in the PR endpoint, archive handler, etc.) will correctly transition workspaces going forward. The backfill only matters for "what about workspaces that already had PRs before this migration?" — that's a one-time concern and the damage of repeated runs outweighs the benefit.

If you want to preserve the one-time backfill benefit, move the three UPDATE statements into the MIGRATIONS array as separate entries, guarded by a pattern that won't re-run. The simplest guard: wrap each in a conditional UPDATE that includes `AND status = 'in-progress'` — wait, that's exactly what they already do, and that's the problem! The condition is true again after a user manual override.

**Final answer:** Remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` entirely — from queries.ts and from database.ts. Delete the function definition and its call. The cost of incorrect repeat runs exceeds the benefit of one-time data migration. The auto-derive logic will handle ongoing transitions correctly.

## Issue #2: WebSocket mutation accepts arbitrary status strings

In `apps/backend/src/services/query-engine.ts`, the `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation arm reads `status` via `readStringParam` and casts with `as WorkspaceStatus` (compile-time only). No runtime Zod validation.

**Fix:** Import `WorkspaceStatusSchema` from `@shared/enums` and parse the status value before passing it to `setWorkspaceStatus`. Change:

```ts
setWorkspaceStatus(workspaceId, status as WorkspaceStatus);
```

To:

```ts
const parsed = WorkspaceStatusSchema.parse(status);
setWorkspaceStatus(workspaceId, parsed);
```

This will throw a Zod error (caught by the mutation handler's try/catch) for invalid values.

Note: `WorkspaceStatusSchema` is already imported in query-engine.ts as a type — check if it needs to be imported as a value import. It's a Zod schema object (a value), so it needs a value import, not just `import type`. Check the current imports.

## Steps

1. Read the current state of both files
2. Remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus` function from `apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts`

Fix two issues in the workspace status feature. Working directory: /Users/zvada/Developer/agent/box-ide/.conductor/da-nang-v3

## Issue #1: Backfill runs on every startup, overrides user-set status

`backfillWorkspaceStatus()` in `apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts` runs on every app startup via `initDatabase()` in `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts`. The SQL `WHERE pr_url IS NOT NULL AND state = 'ready' AND status = 'in-progress'` will match workspaces where a user manually set status back to `in-progress` — overriding their choice.

**Fix:** Remove the `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` call from `initDatabase()` and instead add the backfill SQL statements directly into the `MIGRATIONS` array in `shared/schema.ts`. The MIGRATIONS system already handles idempotency via error catching (`duplicate column` etc). For UPDATE statements that can't fail on re-run, wrap the three backfill UPDATEs into a single compound migration that first checks whether the migration has already run (e.g., by checking if any workspace has a non-default status).

Actually, the simplest and cleanest approach: add the backfill as individual UPDATE statements in the MIGRATIONS array right after the ALTER TABLE that adds the status column. These UPDATEs are naturally one-time because:
- They only run when the ALTER TABLE preceding them succeeds (i.e., when the column is first added)
- On subsequent startups, the ALTER TABLE fails with "duplicate column" and the entire migration loop catches and continues

Wait, looking at the code more carefully: the MIGRATIONS array is a flat list, and each statement runs independently with its own try/catch. So adding backfill UPDATEs after the ALTER TABLE won't make them conditional on the ALTER TABLE.

**Better approach:** Remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` entirely. Instead, use a single compound SQL statement in MIGRATIONS that creates a temp table as a guard:

Actually the simplest: just remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` from database.ts and from queries.ts. Remove the import too. The backfill was nice for initial migration but it's dangerous to run repeatedly. Users with existing databases already had their workspaces set correctly when the ALTER TABLE ran (all defaulted to 'in-progress'). The auto-derive logic (`autoProgressStatus` in the PR endpoint, archive handler, etc.) will correctly transition workspaces going forward. The backfill only matters for "what about workspaces that already had PRs before this migration?" — that's a one-time concern and the damage of repeated runs outweighs the benefit.

If you want to preserve the one-time backfill benefit, move the three UPDATE statements into the MIGRATIONS array as separate entries, guarded by a pattern that won't re-run. The simplest guard: wrap each in a conditional UPDATE that includes `AND status = 'in-progress'` — wait, that's exactly what they already do, and that's the problem! The condition is true again after a user manual override.

**Final answer:** Remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus()` entirely — from queries.ts and from database.ts. Delete the function definition and its call. The cost of incorrect repeat runs exceeds the benefit of one-time data migration. The auto-derive logic will handle ongoing transitions correctly.

## Issue #2: WebSocket mutation accepts arbitrary status strings

In `apps/backend/src/services/query-engine.ts`, the `updateWorkspaceStatus` mutation arm reads `status` via `readStringParam` and casts with `as WorkspaceStatus` (compile-time only). No runtime Zod validation.

**Fix:** Import `WorkspaceStatusSchema` from `@shared/enums` and parse the status value before passing it to `setWorkspaceStatus`. Change:

```ts
setWorkspaceStatus(workspaceId, status as WorkspaceStatus);
```

To:

```ts
const parsed = WorkspaceStatusSchema.parse(status);
setWorkspaceStatus(workspaceId, parsed);
```

This will throw a Zod error (caught by the mutation handler's try/catch) for invalid values.

Note: `WorkspaceStatusSchema` is already imported in query-engine.ts as a type — check if it needs to be imported as a value import. It's a Zod schema object (a value), so it needs a value import, not just `import type`. Check the current imports.

## Steps

1. Read the current state of both files
2. Remove `backfillWorkspaceStatus` function from `apps/backend/src/db/queries.ts`
3. Remove the import and call from `apps/backend/src/lib/database.ts`
4. In `apps/backend/src/services/query-engine.ts`: change `import type { WorkspaceStatus } from "@shared/enums"` to `import { WorkspaceStatusSchema } from "@shared/enums"` (value import, not type-only), and add `.parse()` before the DB write
5. Run `bun run typecheck` to verify
6. Run `bun run test:backend` to verify tests pass

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Typed query functions — centralized DB reads for all route handlers.
     3→ *
     4→ * Every `as` cast lives here (once per query), not in routes.
     5→ * Route handlers call these functions instead of inline SQL.
     6→ */
     7→import type Database from "better-sqlite3";
     8→import type {
     9→  RepositoryRow,
    10→  RepositoryWithCountsRow,
    11→  WorkspaceRow,
    12→  WorkspaceWithDetailsRow,
    13→  SessionRow,
    14→  SessionWithDetailsRow,
    15→  MessageRow,
    16→  StatsRow,
    17→} from "./types";
    18→
    19→// ─── Workspace Queries ───────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→/**
    22→ * Canonical workspace + repo + session JOIN.
    23→ * Single source of truth — reused by list, get-by-id, and by-repo queries.
    24→ */
    25→const WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT = `
    26→  SELECT
    27→    w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    28→    w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.status, w.current_session_id,
    29→    w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    30→    w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    31→    w.updated_at,
    32→    r.name as repo_name, r.root_path, r.git_default_branch,
    33→    s.status as session_status, s.model,
    34→    s.error_category as session_error_category,
    35→    s.error_message as session_error_message,
    36→    s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    37→  FROM workspaces w
    38→  LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    39→  LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    40→`;
    41→
    42→export function getAllWorkspaces(db: Database.Database): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    43→  return db
    44→    .prepare(
    45→      `
    46→    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    47→    ORDER BY w.updated_at DESC
    48→    LIMIT 100
    49→  `
    50→    )
    51→    .all() as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    52→}
    53→
    54→export function getWorkspacesByRepo(
    55→  db: Database.Database,
    56→  state?: string
    57→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[] {
    58→  // Support comma-separated states (e.g. "ready,initializing")
    59→  let stateFilter = "";
    60→  let stateParams: string[] = [];
    61→  if (state) {
    62→    const states = state.split(",").map((s) => s.trim());
    63→    stateFilter = `WHERE w.state IN (${states.map(() => "?").join(",")})`;
    64→    stateParams = states;
    65→  }
    66→  return db
    67→    .prepare(
    68→      `
    69→    SELECT
    70→      w.id, w.repository_id, w.slug, w.title, w.git_branch,
    71→      w.git_target_branch, w.state, w.status, w.current_session_id,
    72→      w.pr_url, w.pr_number,
    73→      w.setup_status, w.error_message, w.init_stage,
    74→      w.updated_at,
    75→      r.name as repo_name, r.sort_order as repo_sort_order, r.root_path,
    76→      r.git_default_branch,
    77→      s.status as session_status, s.model,
    78→      s.error_category as session_error_category,
    79→      s.error_message as session_error_message,
    80→      s.last_user_message_at as latest_message_sent_at
    81→    FROM workspaces w
    82→    LEFT JOIN repositories r ON w.repository_id = r.id
    83→    LEFT JOIN sessions s ON w.current_session_id = s.id
    84→    ${stateFilter}
    85→    ORDER BY r.sort_order, r.name, w.updated_at DESC
    86→  `
    87→    )
    88→    .all(...stateParams) as WorkspaceWithDetailsRow[];
    89→}
    90→
    91→export function getWorkspaceById(
    92→  db: Database.Database,
    93→  id: string
    94→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined {
    95→  return db
    96→    .prepare(
    97→      `
    98→    ${WORKSPACE_DETAILS_SELECT}
    99→    WHERE w.id = ?
   100→  `
   101→    )

   105→/** Used by withWorkspace middleware — lighter query without session JOIN. */
   106→export function getWorkspaceForMiddleware(
   107→  db: Database.Database,

   109→): WorkspaceWithDetailsRow | undefined {
   110→  return db
   111→    .prepare(

   114→    FROM workspaces w
